title: Brazil’s Securities Regulator Forms Task Force for Tokenization Framework
summary: Brazil’s securities regulator has established a task force to create a tokenization framework within 60 days. The proposal will address key issues like ownership records, private key custody, and transaction reversibility.
body:
Brazil’s Securities and Exchange Commission (CVM) has set up a task force to develop a comprehensive framework for tokenization within the next 60 days. The initiative aims to address critical issues such as official ownership records, private key custody, transaction reversibility, and system liability. This move is part of Brazil’s broader push to integrate blockchain technology into its financial infrastructure.

The task force will focus on creating clear guidelines for the tokenization of assets, ensuring that the process is secure and transparent. Key areas of concern include determining how ownership of tokenized assets will be recorded and managed, as well as establishing protocols for the custody of private keys. The framework will also address the reversibility of transactions, a critical aspect for consumer protection and dispute resolution.

This development is significant for everyday investors and businesses, as it could pave the way for a more secure and regulated tokenization market in Brazil. For users, this means potentially easier and safer access to tokenized assets, with clear rules on ownership and liability. It also sets the stage for broader adoption of blockchain technology in the country’s financial sector.

Investors and businesses should watch for the task force’s proposal, expected within the next 60 days. The outcome could shape the future of tokenization in Brazil, making it a key development for anyone interested in digital assets. Stay tuned for updates as the task force works towards its deadline.
twitterThread: Brazil’s securities regulator just launched a task force to create a tokenization framework in 60 days. Key focus: ownership records, private key custody, and transaction reversibility.
This move could make tokenized assets safer and more accessible for everyday investors. The framework will address critical issues like who holds the private keys and how disputes are resolved.

The proposal is due in 60 days. If you’re into digital assets, keep an eye on this—it could reshape Brazil’s tokenization market. Read more → https://www.cryptocatalyst.news/articles/2026-07-20-brazils-securities-regulator-forms-task-force-for-tokenization-framework
